preset_description: Dark = decision signal. Emphasis accumulates toward conversion — trust anchors and commitment moments are dark, top-of-funnel is light.

accent_tokens._note: Brand primary CTA color. Global — not per-preset. Two variants: button on a light/neutral section vs. on a dark section.

sections.name: Navigation
sections.rationale: NavigationHeader is required on every page. For a Validator archetype at the conversion funnel stage, the minimal variant is ideal — strip distracting nav links and keep only the logo and a single persistent CTA (e.g., 'Start Plan'). This reduces decision fatigue for founders ready to commit and keeps the conversion action visible at all times.

sections.name: Hero — Validation-Led Value Proposition
sections.rationale: Although HeroPrimary lists decision (not conversion) as its deepest funnel stage, it does not qualify under Rule 3. However, CTABanner qualifies for both Validator and conversion. At the conversion stage, founders already understand the product — the opening must reaffirm their decision with a trust-anchored headline and a clear conversion CTA. The Validator variant of CTABanner with subhead and trust_signal ('No credit card required', '14-day free trial') serves as a hero-weight opening that validates the purchase decision immediately above the fold.

sections.name: Pricing Plans
sections.rationale: PricingCard is explicitly designed for the conversion funnel stage and the Validator archetype. Early-stage SaaS founders are cost-sensitive and need transparent, justified pricing. The Validator variant with full descriptions and detailed feature lists lets them verify value-for-money before committing. Using the highlighted variant on the recommended plan gives a clear default choice, reducing friction for Validators who want to feel they've made the rational selection.

sections.name: Outcome Proof — Key Metrics
sections.rationale: StatBlock qualifies for Validator at the decision/conversion boundary. Placing attributed, sourced metrics directly after pricing validates the investment founders are about to make — e.g., '3.2x average ROI in 90 days (source: internal cohort data)'. Validators at the conversion stage need quantitative reassurance that the price they just reviewed delivers measurable outcomes. Source attribution is critical per component notes.

sections.name: Trust Testimonial — Peer Founder Validation
sections.rationale: TestimonialSingle is the primary trust signal for the Validator archetype at the decision stage, placed here to bridge the gap between pricing review and final conversion. A single, high-credibility testimonial from a named early-stage founder (with photo, role, and company) placed near the final CTA converts Validators by providing peer-validated social proof at the exact decision moment. This is the most persuasive placement per component notes.

sections.name: Credibility Bar — Logo Social Proof
sections.rationale: SocialProofBar qualifies for Validator across all funnel stages including conversion (via decision adjacency). The Validator variant leads with a featured quote and logos below, reinforcing that recognizable companies trust this product. For early-stage founders at conversion, seeing peer companies or known accelerator logos removes the last layer of perceived risk before they commit.

sections.name: Final Conversion CTA
sections.rationale: CTABanner is explicitly designed for both the Validator archetype and the conversion funnel stage. This terminal-position banner captures the action after all trust and value proof has been delivered. The Validator variant includes a subhead reinforcing the risk-reversal message (e.g., 'Cancel anytime. No contracts.') and a trust_signal, giving founders the final rational permission to convert. The split variant works well here — directional copy on the left summarizing why, CTA on the right capturing the action.

sections.name: Footer
sections.rationale: FooterMinimal is required on every page. The with-cta variant is essential on this conversion page — it recaptures departing Validator users who scrolled past all content without converting. For early-stage founders who are methodical and may hesitate, a recovery CTA in the footer provides one last low-pressure opportunity to start their paid plan.

page.reasoning: minimal variant for conversion-focused pages — strip nav_links, keep logo and single CTA only. The persistent CTA in nav is the most converted element on most SaaS pages.
Validator variant: include subhead and trust_signal (e.g., 'No credit card required'). centered layout supports validation-led messaging for early-stage founders evaluating conversion risk.

page.reasoning: Always include role and company for Validator credibility. with-photo increases engagement and perceived authenticity for peer founder validation.

slots.quote: We cut our design-to-dev handoff from three weeks to four days. MODE's intent-aware components meant our engineer didn't have to guess — every state was already built. We shipped our first paid tier six weeks ahead of schedule.

page.reasoning: Validator variant leads with quote, logos below. This establishes credibility through both founder voice and recognizable brand logos for validator archetype.

slots.headline: Trusted by founders from leading accelerators and early-stage studios
slots.quote: MODE is the first design system I've used that actually respects the constraints of a two-person team. It made us look like we had a full design org from day one.

page.reasoning: Validator variant includes description and longer feature list. highlighted variant for recommended plan to guide early-stage founders toward conversion decision.

slots.description: Everything an early-stage SaaS team needs to build, ship, and iterate — without hiring a senior designer.
features: 1 team seat included, add more at $19/seat

page.reasoning: Validator variant: include subhead and trust_signal (e.g., 'No credit card required'). Terminal action for conversion funnel requires trust reassurance for validator archetype.

slots.subhead: Join 2,100+ SaaS founders who stopped rebuilding UI from scratch. Cancel anytime. No contracts. Full access from day one.
slots.trust_signal: No credit card required · Cancel anytime · SOC 2 Type II compliant
